messages.content:
What is an important conclusion of climate prediction models?


Accuracy in climate change reporting by businesses is impossible.


Reporting on climate change is often avoided by businesses.


Temperature changes under two degrees Celsius are insignificant.


Increase in global temperature will continue, but can be reduced.
Increase in global temperature will continue, but can be reduced.

How can myNav Green Cloud Advisor help an Accenture client achieve a clear emissions profile for their data centers?


by applying a single green solution plan to all clients regardless of location or readiness


by establishing the client's existing data center energy consumption for comparison


by using special software to e-design data centers with renewable energy sources


by allowing the client to retrieve and store the largest quantities of data possible
by establishing the client's existing data center energy consumption for comparison

A customer purchases a new phone from an online store. Although the customer did not create the phone themselves, there are still carbon emissions that result from producing the phone in a factory and shipping it overseas to the customer.

Which type of emissions are described in this example?


Scope Two


Scope Three


Scope One


Scope Zero
Scope Three

In addition to environmental issues, what does true sustainability address?


increasing data resources and security


preserving social and economic resources


reducing the costs of new technologies


increasing revenue and profitability
preserving social and economic resources

What is a benefit of a company publishing a sustainability report?


to provide information on its financial stability


to publicize local environmental regulations


to describe its environmental goals and progress


to describe its overall goals for economic growth
to describe its environmental goals and progress

How can features of blockchain support sustainability efforts?


Supply chain data can help companies provide transparency into social issues.


Blockchain technologies can reduce a company's overall computing power.


Blockchain technologies can improve a company's energy footprint.


Access to data can improve marketing and reduce costs to growth markets.
Supply chain data can help companies provide transparency into social issues.

Where do most of a software-focused company's carbon emissions typically come from?


finance and accounting


public relations and marketing


corporate operations


product manufacturing
corporate operations

A large factory is monitoring machinery with digital sensors and collecting the data.

How can this data directly impact the factory's sustainability goals?


Factory staff can use the data to schedule time more efficiently to reduce payroll expenses.


Factory employees can use data to track production trends over a specified period of time.


The data will help the factory engage with local manufacturers about supply and demand.


The data provides real-time information to allow the factory to quickly adjust resources.
The data provides real-time information to allow the factory to quickly adjust resources.

What does it mean when a company talks about being carbon neutral?


It will eliminate carbon emissions by switching entirely to renewable sources.


It will capture and store atmospheric carbon dioxide generated by their factories.


It will balance out its carbon emissions by reducing or offsetting them.


It will stop the production of all the items that are increasing its carbon footprint.
It will balance out its carbon emissions by reducing or offsetting them.

How can edge computing be used to improve sustainability?


by using sensors to track real-time data about energy usage


by instantly converting older machines to use renewable energy


by requiring all devices to connect directly to government servers


by allowing devices to operate without a network connection
by using sensors to track real-time data about energy usage

What is one of the biggest challenges when companies are committed to sustainability?


Employees need to have guidelines about how new changes will impact their salaries.


Operations are impacted the most due to a reduction in procurement.


There is a mental shift to consider sustainability in all aspects of the business.


The surrounding community needs to understand the environmental impact of the changes.
There is a mental shift to consider sustainability in all aspects of the business.

Accenture is one of several hundred companies that has signed on to the United Nations Global Compact (UNGC) Business Ambition pledge.

What is the goal of this pledge?


to donate to charity in exchange for exemption from regulations


to keep global warming below a 1.5 Celsius increase from pre-industrial levels


to switch entirely to solar power at all office locations worldwide


to reverse all effects of global warming within the next decade
to keep global warming below a 1.5 Celsius increase from pre-industrial levels

A user wants to visualize a highly complex 3D model using a Virtual Reality headset.

What is the best way for the user to do this?


Use a cardboard viewer and run the application from a mobile phone.


Use a self-contained headset and lower the headset's frame rate.


Use a tethered headset connected to a powerful PC.


Use any headset and increase the bandwidth of the Wi-Fi connection.
Use a tethered headset connected to a powerful PC.

What is a key factor in the accelerated use of Extended Reality applications in business?


an increased need to motivate employees to return to the office


a desire to completely replace conventional desktop and laptop computers


a need for protection against cyber attacks and privacy breaches


a growing emphasis on cost savings and sustainable business practices
a growing emphasis on cost savings and sustainable business practices

When it comes to Extended Reality (XR) in business, what fact differentiates Accenture from other companies?


Accenture offers a one-size-fits-all set of XR services to all clients regardless of industry.


Accenture has designed its own exclusive Virtual Reality headset to share with our diamond clients.


Accenture has acquired Microsoft's XR division to create a new platform which will change the way businesses interact.


Accenture and Microsoft are currently the only two companies who can provide and support a holistic XR service.
Accenture and Microsoft are currently the only two companies who can provide and support a holistic XR service.

What is one reason a fast frame rate is important in Virtual Reality?


to allow objects to be manipulated by hand


to minimize the time needed to finish a game


to reduce an application's loading time


to reduce the risk of motion sickness
to reduce the risk of motion sickness

How could a travel and tourism company utilize Virtual Reality to enhance their business?


by removing limitations to travel imposed by language barriers


by collating search histories to recommend potential travel destinations


by providing potential travelers with an immersive view of destinations


by promoting events at tourist attractions across social media channels
by providing potential travelers with an immersive view of destinations

What is the screen door effect?


when a low-resolution display creates a pixelated image


when the boundaries of the user's peripheral vision expand


when a broader viewing range results in a more detailed image


when high latency causes motion sickness in the viewer
when a low-resolution display creates a pixelated image

What enables a Virtual Reality headset to create a three-dimensional perspective for the user?


using exceptionally high video resolution


tracking the user's location with GPS technology


displaying slightly different views for each eye


being permanently connected to the internet
displaying slightly different views for each eye

What does the frame rate of a Virtual Reality headset indicate?


the resolution and quality of the display


the number of users connected to the application


the number of images displayed per second


the strength of the device's internet connection
the number of images displayed per second

The newest model of a car features technology that scans the surrounding environment and projects information through an interface on the windshield, which the driver can then interact with.

Which type of technology would this be an example of?


Mixed Reality


Augmented Reality


Alternate Reality


Virtual Reality
Augmented Reality

A client in the manufacturing industry approaches Accenture with an interest in using Extended Reality (XR) for their business.

What is a unique way Accenture could demonstrate our XR capabilities to this client?


Take the client through a virtual space built to reflect the client's specific industry.


Show the client a standardized Virtual Reality offering built identically for all industries.


Connect the client with an external vendor who can build them a fully customized solution.


Provide the client with state-of-the-art Virtual Reality headsets free of charge.
Take the client through a virtual space built to reflect the client's specific industry.

What is a potential benefit of applying Extended Reality solutions to surgical science?


It allows the visualization of vital information in real time during surgical procedures.


It estimates the anesthesia required by calculating a patient's body mass index (BMI).


It predicts the amount of healing time needed based on medication and scans.


It monitors hygiene by tracking contact between patients and hospital staff.
It allows the visualization of vital information in real time during surgical procedures.

A construction-based client would like to develop an application that can analyze an image of machinery and overlay information that will assist in the repair.

Which form of Extended Reality can best assist the client in this situation?


Mixed Reality


Virtual Reality


Augmented Reality


Haptic Reality
Augmented Reality

How have Cloud and 5G helped make Extended Reality (XR) technology more accessible to a wide audience?


by requiring users to upgrade their headsets with new graphic processing units


by using blockchain databases to track and manage user activity within XR environments


by providing a more flexible and affordable way to render quality images at high speeds


by making XR applications and devices increasingly reliant on tethered connections
by providing a more flexible and affordable way to render quality images at high speeds

What does the "mesh" do in Augmented Reality?


It allows effects to be added in the room or on the objects.


It creates realistic-looking objects within a virtual world.


It displays images as if viewed through a screen door.


It provides a filter to overlay digital images on top of objects.
It allows effects to be added in the room or on the objects.

What is a characteristic of Augmented Reality?


It can only be used with a specialized headset.


It requires the user to have a tethered connection to a computer.


It overlays digital content on the user's real-world view.


It replaces the user's entire field of view with a virtual world.
It overlays digital content on the user's real-world view.

Why is quantum computing useful for optimization problems?


It uses human intuition to only explore certain potential solutions.


It divides the intractable complexity into bits to calculate simple solutions.


It evaluates solutions one by one in a sequential manner.


It can perform operations on a combination of all possible solutions.
It can perform operations on a combination of all possible solutions.

Which part of the drug discovery life cycle can quantum computing impact the most?


regulatory approval


laboratory testing


molecular simulation


clinical trials
molecular simulation

Which statement describes the current availability of quantum computers?


They are widely available and affordable in consumer retail stores.


They will be developed for home installation within the next decade.


They are owned by a few companies, but available to many.


They are currently only available on certain mobile devices.
They are owned by a few companies, but available to many.

What is a benefit of interference in quantum computing?


It reinforces certain results and helps identify good paths.


It helps to untangle qubits so that they reveal better paths.


It allows for encryption of data within large databases.


It disrupts repeatable processes and allows for automation.
It reinforces certain results and helps identify good paths.

What is the term for breaking a larger number apart into smaller numbers that can be multiplied together to get a specific result?


interference


entanglement


factorization


superposition
factorization

Which term refers to the strong information correlation between qubits?


entanglement


coherence


quantum annealing


linear combination
entanglement

Which term refers to the strong information correlation between qubits?


entanglement


coherence


quantum annealing


linear combination
entanglement

What is an example of using quantum computing for sustainable practices?


storing large computing servers in high-altitude conditions


improving the efficiency of batteries on electric cars


installing edge computing devices in common public places


mining cryptocurrency using multiple connected computers
improving the efficiency of batteries on electric cars

How is a qubit in quantum computing different from a regular bit in classical computing?


A qubit takes up less memory space than a regular bit.


A qubit can represent an indeterminate state.


A qubit stores information as graphical images.


A qubit can represent a value as 0, 1, or 2.
A qubit can represent an indeterminate state.

Why might businesses be interested in using quantum computers?


to solve intractable problems


to create graphics for immersive environments


to run mobile apps more quickly


to process extremely large data sets
to solve intractable problems

What is meant by "applied quantum computing?"


using quantum computers to solve real business problems


installing quantum computers directly in company offices


building more advanced quantum computing hardware


processing simple mathematical functions more quickly
using quantum computers to solve real business problems

How is Accenture addressing the emerging market for quantum computing technology?


by advising our clients to replace all their classical computers with quantum machines


by taking a "wait and see" approach until quantum computers are developed further


by adopting a Quantum-as-a-Service approach using existing quantum computers


by building our own quantum computers that will be hosted in Accenture offices
by adopting a Quantum-as-a-Service approach using existing quantum computers

Why is quantum computing potentially a better fit for weather forecasting than classical computers?


It can perform advanced simulations more efficiently.


It can be easily installed at locations around the globe.


It can function efficiently when stored at high temperatures.


It can store extensive data for better pattern recognition.
It can perform advanced simulations more efficiently.
